rules.description: For topology Go boards, count liberties and territories with graph flood-fill over the actual adjacency, replacing corner/side concepts with region boundary size and graph cut value.
Do not copy normal joseki or fuseki onto torus, cylinder, polar, Mobius, RP2, 3D, or 4D boards; learn topology-specific books from self-play and use only abstract shape and liberty features.

Transfer local Go ideas such as double-atari, eye making, ladders, nets, one-point jumps, knight moves, large knight moves, and pulling moves through graph paths and graph cuts instead of board-screen geometry.

Generalize 金角銀邊草肚皮 by replacing literal corners/sides/center with hard-boundary anchors, seam cycles, graph centrality, and region boundary length. On closed boards, do not invent corner bonuses where no hard corner exists.
On topology boards, pass when graph flood-fill shows remaining empty regions are already owned and point plays would only reduce own graph-area score.
For 3D and surface-embedded Go, liberties must come only from graph neighbors. Prefer moves that reduce opponent group escape volume while keeping own groups connected through high-degree junctions.

On torus, cylinder, Mobius, sphere, and 3D boards, opening moves should estimate region control across seams and avoid treating rendered edges as physical edges unless the graph actually has hard boundaries.
